Title: Overexpression of TaSTT3b-2B improves resistance to sharp eyespot and increases grain weight in wheat

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: STAUROSPORINE AND TEMPERATURE SENSITIVE3 (STT3) catalytic subunit of oligosaccharyltransferase; N-glycosylation-related transmembrane protein with 11 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Rhizoctonia cerealis infection; strongly induced 1–8 h after exogenous JA treatment; higher expression in resistant cultivars and in varieties with higher thousand-grain weight
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in transgenic overexpression lines than WT; naturally induced in infected leaf sheaths after R. cerealis infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in transgenic wheat; BSMV-VIGS silencing; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; JA quantification by HPLC-MS/MS; exogenous MeJA rescue; DIECA inhibition; subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of three TaSTT3b-2B overexpression lines vs WT at 4 dpi with R. cerealis; accession NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Jasmonic acid; starch/sucrose-associated grain filling products
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid supports immunity to necrotrophic fungus; grain-filling carbohydrates support increased grain weight
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf sheaths for JA measurement; grain/endosperm for grain weight-related carbohydrate deposition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizoctonia cerealis infection; exogenous MeJA; DIECA pretreatment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize Ubi promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for localization assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaSTT3b-2B is strongly induced by R. cerealis and JA, and its transcript abundance correlates with sharp eyespot resistance. 2. Overexpression enhances resistance to R. cerealis, elevates JA content, and increases grain length, width, and thousand-grain weight, whereas silencing compromises resistance. 3. TaSTT3b-2B upregulates JA biosynthesis-related genes and grain size-related genes including starch synthase and sucrose synthase genes, and JA treatment rescues the silencing phenotype.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid accumulation, enhances resistance to sharp eyespot, and increases grain size and thousand-grain weight under R. cerealis infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty in spike number per m2, spike length, spikelet number per spike, or grain number per spike was reported; broader multi-environment validation was suggested
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for wheat breeding to simultaneously improve sharp eyespot resistance and grain weight; overexpression or allele mining/editing of high-expression variants may be useful, but multi-site validation is needed
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core experimental focus is TaSTT3b-2B rather than other STT3 homologs. It likely acts upstream of JA biosynthesis and defense/grain-weight gene expression, but not as a transcription factor or metabolic enzyme directly producing JA or starch.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Weakly upregulated at 10 and 21 dpi with R. cerealis infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSMV-VIGS silencing; qRT-PCR; disease phenotyping after R. cerealis inoculation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaSTT3a was identified as a wheat STT3 homolog with modest pathogen-induced expression. 2. Silencing TaSTT3a increased lesion area after R. cerealis infection. 3. Compared with TaSTT3b, TaSTT3a had only a minor contribution to resistance and was not linked to nutrient metabolite regulation in this study.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor positive contribution to wheat resistance to R. cerealis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority than TaSTT3b-2B for breeding based on this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimental gene in results, but no direct nutrient metabolite causality was established.
